# `nestedCombine` Function

Combine dimensions into a nested map

Produces the cartesian product of the input dimensions and returns the result as a nested map instead of a flat path-keyed map.

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
nestedCombine(input dynamic, separator string...) dynamic
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `input` (Dynamic) Ordered list of {key, values} items to combine. Each item is an object with a 'key' string and a 'values' list or map.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `separator` (Variadic, String) Separator used to split path keys when expanding. Defaults to provider separator.

## Example Usage

### Basic usage

`nestedCombine` is like `combine`, but returns a nested map instead of a flat one. Each dimension becomes a level of nesting, and the leaf objects contain all dimension values as attributes.

```terraform
locals {
  dimensions = [
    { key = "env",     items = ["dev", "prod"] },
    { key = "region",  items = ["europe-west1", "europe-north1"] },
    { key = "service", items = ["api", "web"] },
  ]
}

output "nested_combinations" {
  value = provider::util::nestedCombine(local.dimensions)
}
# Result structure:
# {
#   dev = {
#     europe-west1  = { api = { env = "dev", region = "europe-west1",  service = "api" }
#                       web = { env = "dev", region = "europe-west1",  service = "web" } }
#     europe-north1 = { api = { env = "dev", region = "europe-north1", service = "api" }
#                       web = { env = "dev", region = "europe-north1", service = "web" } }
#   }
#   prod = { ... }
# }
```

### Selecting a slice of the result

Because the result is nested, you can select a specific subtree directly.

```terraform
locals {
  all = provider::util::nestedCombine([
    { key = "env",     items = ["dev", "stage", "prod"] },
    { key = "service", items = ["api", "web", "worker"] },
  ])
}

# All services for the dev environment
output "dev_services" {
  value = local.all["dev"]
}

# Just the prod/api entry
output "prod_api" {
  value = local.all["prod"]["api"]
}
```

### Comparison to `combine`

Use `combine` when you need a flat map for `for_each`. Use `nestedCombine` when you want to access specific subtrees by index, or when passing results to `nestedFilter`.

```terraform
locals {
  dims = [
    { key = "env",    items = ["dev", "prod"] },
    { key = "region", items = ["europe-west1", "us-central1"] },
  ]
}

# Flat map — good for for_each
output "flat" {
  value = provider::util::combine(local.dims)
}

# Nested map — good for indexed access or nestedFilter
output "nested" {
  value = provider::util::nestedCombine(local.dims)
}
```
